description:
You are an MLOps engineer who must periodically verify that the machines 
    listed in an experiment-tracking configuration file can actually be reached 
    through DNS or the local hosts database.  
    To automate this check you have decided to keep a tiny audit log in your 
    home directory.

    Goal
    ----
    1. Create two sub-directories under /home/user/mlops  
       • /home/user/mlops/config  
       • /home/user/mlops/logs  

    2. Inside /home/user/mlops/config create a plain-text file called 
       /home/user/mlops/config/experiment_hosts.txt that **contains exactly** the 
       following two lines (no additional blank lines or spaces):
           localhost
           invalid.host

    3. Read each hostname in the order it appears in experiment_hosts.txt, try to
       resolve it to an IPv4 address, and immediately append the result to the 
       log file /home/user/mlops/logs/dns_audit.log in the CSV format described 
       below.

    4. The dns_audit.log file must already contain a header row before the first
       data line is written.  The header row and every subsequent data row must
       follow this exact, comma-separated, **four-column** schema:

           timestamp,hostname,resolved_ip,status

       • timestamp  : The current UTC time in strict ISO-8601 format  
                     (e.g. 2022-12-31T23:59:59Z).  
       • hostname   : The hostname taken from the list file.  
       • resolved_ip: The first IPv4 address that was returned.  
                     If no IPv4 address is obtained write the literal “N/A”.  
       • status     : SUCCESS if at least one IPv4 address was obtained,  
                     otherwise FAIL.

       A fully-populated log file produced by a **single** run over the current
       experiment_hosts.txt must therefore look exactly like this template
       (timestamps will of course differ in a real run):

           timestamp,hostname,resolved_ip,status
           2022-12-31T23:59:59Z,localhost,127.0.0.1,SUCCESS
           2022-12-31T23:59:59Z,invalid.host,N/A,FAIL

       • The first data line (localhost) must come before the second data line 
         (invalid.host) – ordering matters.
       • Use a single newline character (`\n`) to terminate every row including 
         the very last one, so that the file ends with a trailing newline.

    5. The final terminal output of your session should show the absolute path to
       the fresh log file so that a human can quickly open it, but you do NOT 
       need to print the file’s contents to STDOUT.

    Hints (do NOT reveal commands)
    ------------------------------
    • Standard tools such as “getent hosts”, “dig +short”, or “nslookup” are all
      acceptable ways to resolve a name; pick one that is available.  
    • Remember to create the parent directories before trying to write files in
      them.  
    • A typical invocation sequence will involve about 5-10 shell commands in
      total, so keep your solution short and tidy.

    Deliverable that the automated grader will check
    ------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/mlops/config/experiment_hosts.txt exists and contains exactly 
       the two hostnames shown above (one per line, no extra spaces or lines).
    2. /home/user/mlops/logs/dns_audit.log exists and  
       • Has four comma-separated columns,  
       • Contains a single header row followed by exactly two data rows,  
       • The first data row is for “localhost” with resolved_ip 127.0.0.1 and 
         status SUCCESS,  
       • The second data row is for “invalid.host” with resolved_ip N/A and 
         status FAIL,  
       • Each row (including the last) ends with a newline character.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent begins:
    -------------------------------------------
    • No /home/user/mlops directory is present.

    Expected end state used for automated verification:
    ---------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory structure  
       /home/user/mlops/  
       ├── config/  
       │   └── experiment_hosts.txt  
       └── logs/  
           └── dns_audit.log  

    2. /home/user/mlops/config/experiment_hosts.txt  
       Exact contents (including trailing newline):  
       «localhost\ninvalid.host\n»

    3. /home/user/mlops/logs/dns_audit.log  
       • First line (header) must be literally:  
         «timestamp,hostname,resolved_ip,status\n»  
       • Second line must match this regex (note the comma-separated groups):  
         «[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z,localhost,127\.0\.0\.1,SUCCESS\n»  
       • Third line must match this regex:  
         «[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z,invalid\.host,N/A,FAIL\n»  

    4. The dns_audit.log file must contain exactly three lines (header + 2 data
       rows) and conclude with a newline.  

    5. Permissions: Files created by the agent inherit default user umask; no
       special permission checks will be enforced, only existence and contents.
